response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer, but the profile does not match this role's mandatory requirements. His C++ experience is limited to a ~7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a thesis project, far short of the required 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. Database experience centers on MongoDB and MySQL rather than SQL Server/Oracle, and there is no Fixed Income or capital-markets domain background — RIDE Capital was a German investment-administration fintech, not an investment-banking or fixed-income environment. The gap on every mandatory axis makes an interview hard to justify despite solid general engineering credentials.
strengths: Broad, sustained senior-level engineering experience across backend, systems, and low-latency work (DLang async services at millions of requests/sec)
Strong software craftsmanship practices: TDD, CI/CD, pairing, trunk-based development, plus open-source authorship
Excellent communication skills evidenced by workshops, conference talks, and teaching courses
concerns: Minimal professional C++ experience (~7 months in 2014–2015), not on Windows-focused financial systems, versus a mandatory 5–10 years
No Fixed Income, derivatives, or capital-markets/investment-banking domain exposure; no Summit platform experience
SQL experience is MySQL/MongoDB oriented with no stated SQL Server or Oracle background

key_factors.factor: Insufficient C++ depth vs. mandatory requirement
Strong general engineering fundamentals and communication
